label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out step by step. So the question is asking whether following a series of movement instructions will bring you back to the starting point. The instructions are: take 3 steps, turn around, take 5 steps, turn right, turn right, take 1 step, take 1 step. The options are Yes or No. First, I need to visualize the path. Let me imagine I'm standing at the starting point, facing a certain direction, maybe north. Let's assume the initial direction is north unless specified otherwise. The first instruction is to take 3 steps. If I'm facing north, I move 3 steps north. Then, turn around. Turning around means facing the opposite direction. So if I was facing north, turning around would face me south. So now I'm facing south. Next is taking 5 steps. Since I'm facing south now, I move 5 steps south. So from the starting point, I first went 3 steps north, then 5 steps south. So the net movement from the starting point now is 5 - 3 = 2 steps south. So I'm 2 steps south of the starting point. Then the instructions say to turn right. If I'm facing south, turning right would make me face west. Then another turn right: from west, turning right would face me north. So after two right turns, I'm facing north again. Then
